content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 18, 1991

Roger Brown
Law Offices of
Lawrence A. Haun
P.O. Box 1056
Sonora, CA 95370

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-106

Dear Mr. Brown:

You have requested advice on behalf of your clients BKK
Corporation and Greenfield Environmental concerning the lobbying
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

QUESTIONS

1. How should Greenfield Environmental and BKK Corporation
be registered and file reports under the Act's lobbying disclosure
provisions?

2. Is Kathy Fletcher a lobbying firm or an in-house
lobbyist?

CONCLUSIONS

1. Both Greenfield Environmental and BKK Corporation must
be identified as Ms. Fletcher's lobbyist employer. The companies
should file consolidated lobbying statements. Please refer to the
Analysis for instructions on registration requirements.

2. Ms. Fletcher is an "in-house" lobbyist.

_______________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

FACTS

Greenfield Environmental and BKK Corporation are affiliated
entities. The companies have common shareholders, directors and
managers. The lobbying efforts of both companies are coordinated.

Kathy Fletcher is an employee of Greenfield Environmental and
provides lobbying services on behalf of Greenfield Environmental
and BKK Corporation. Ms. Fletcher works at the direction of the
common management. BKK Corporation reimburses Greenfield
Environmental for a portion of Ms. Fletcher's salary. Each
company reimburses Ms. Fletcher for its own expenses.

ANALYSIS

You indicated that Greenfield Environmental employs Ms.
Fletcher as a salaried employee and that its affiliate, BKK
Corporation reimburses Greenfield Environmental for a portion of
her salary. In such situations, we have advised that a
corporation and its affiliated entity should file a single
lobbyist employer report. (Savage Advice Letter, No. A-86-103,
copy enclosed.) The "Name of Filer" should be identified as
"Greenfield Environmental and its affiliate, BKK Corporation."
Under these circumstances, Ms. Fletcher will be deemed an in-house
lobbyist.

As an employer of an in-house lobbyist, Greenfield
Environmental and its affiliate, BKK Corporation, must register
with the Secretary of State by filing Form 603 (Lobbyist Employer
Registration). A Form 604 (Lobbyist Certification) signed by Ms.
Fletcher must accompany the Form 603. (Section 86100(c).)
Accompanying these statements should be a letter informing the
Secretary of State that these forms replace the lobbying firm
registration forms filed by Ms. Fletcher.

I have enclosed a set of lobbying forms for your use.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions concerning this matter.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
